The package risks spending the pre-audit window building automation the auditor doesn't need until the Type II observation period.
Postgres connection/pgaudit log ingestion cost is acknowledged but not capped by default — chatty app connection pools can generate surprising Log Analytics bills.
required_changes: Split into three phases with a hard gate. Phase 1 (weeks 1-2, before anything else): all five quick wins — HR-roster-to-Entra/GitHub/pg_roles reconciliation, ALTER ROLE VALID UNTIL on human DB accounts, contractor account expirations, connection-log alerting, and the named-systems offboarding checklist with 24h SLA. The checklist becomes the Type I control of record. Phase 2 (pre-audit): Postgres Entra-auth pilot with 2-3 named contractors for 2 weeks (validate DBeaver/psql/ORM tooling and token-lifetime ergonomics) before any forced cutover; keep one break-glass local pg admin in a vault. Phase 3 (post-Type I / Type II runway): Lifecycle Workflows + SCIM.

Cut SCIM scope from ~15 apps to the 4-6 downstream apps that actually touch customer data or production; the rest stay on the manual checklist. Require vendor tier/pricing quotes for those apps before committing the budget number.

Defer GitHub Enterprise Cloud entirely for now. Substitute a scheduled script (GitHub API) that reconciles org members against the HR roster weekly and flags/removes leavers, plus an org-level PAT max-lifetime policy. Revisit Enterprise when R3/R4 GitHub Actions work happens so we only disrupt CI/CD once.

Take the budget licensing path as the baseline, not the fallback: Logic App/Graph leaver runbook triggered by the HR ticket + Entra ID P2 scoped to the ~80 reviewed users (~$8.6k/yr). Upgrade to the full Governance add-on only if Microsoft confirms scoped licensing at an acceptable price in writing.

Because employeeLeaveDateTime is manually maintained, add a compensating weekly automated reconciliation of the HR roster against enabled Entra accounts (a 50-line script) — this is the actual safety net, make it a named control, not an afterthought.
Change access-review fallback for the Postgres-access group and Azure RBAC from auto-remove to escalate-to-IT-Manager; keep auto-remove for guest/contractor accounts only.
Set a Log Analytics daily ingestion cap on day one and exclude application-pool connection chatter from pgaudit scope (log human/contractor roles only).
Assign a named weekly rotation (me + 2 sysadmins) for alert triage and put the quarterly runbook test on the calendar now with the SOC 2 evidence folder as its output location.

adjusted_effort.cost_estimate_usd: $10k-30k/yr recurring on the phased plan: Entra P2 for ~80 reviewed users ~$8.6k (baseline) or Governance add-on up to ~$17k; Log Analytics $1.5-3k with quota cap; SaaS SCIM/SSO tier uplifts for 4-6 priority apps, unpriced today, budget $0-8k; GitHub Enterprise (~$15k/yr, not $12k) deferred out of this package. One-time labor ~9 person-weeks spread over 4-5 calendar months given SOC 2 load and interrupts — Phase 1 quick wins are ~1 week and near-zero dollars.

feasibility.sequencing_note: Phase 1 quick wins jump the queue: the roster reconciliation and contractor Postgres cleanup are days of work, close a live hole, and generate the cheapest SOC 2 evidence of any of the five packages — do them in week 1 regardless of other priorities. The documented offboarding checklist is what the Type I auditor actually samples, so it must exist well before the audit date; the Lifecycle Workflow/SCIM automation is Type II material and belongs after Type I, when the observation window starts. Relative to the other four mitigations: this lands second overall — behind phishing-resistant MFA/Conditional Access (which protects live accounts, a bigger attack surface than dormant ones, and which this package explicitly leans on), but ahead of EDR/Intune completion and WAF work. Critically, the Postgres Entra-auth migration and any GitHub plan change must be co-scheduled with the R3/R4 secrets/service-principal/managed-identity work — they touch the same connection strings and the same GitHub Actions pipelines, and I refuse to destabilize CI/CD twice. Budget roughly one sysadmin at 50% for a quarter, with my two other packages time-sliced around it.
